title: Smoky Eastern Frittata
description: A smoky twist on the classic frittata, inspired by the flavors of the Middle East.
instructions: In a large skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of oil over medium heat.
Add the sliced frittata and cook until lightly browned, about 3-4 minutes.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Add the chopped parsley and dill to the bowl and stir to combine.
Pour the egg mixture over the cooked frittata in the skillet.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the eggs are almost set.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 8-10 minutes, until the eggs are set.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes.
Use a spatula to loosen the edges of the frittata from the skillet.
Slide the frittata out of the skillet onto a plate and serve hot.
